name: Chess
description: Classic 2-player chess with all standard rules — castling, en passant, pawn promotion, check, checkmate, and stalemate detection. Arcadified with neon visuals and flashy animations.
rules:
OVERVIEW
Standard two-player chess. White moves first. Checkmate wins; stalemate, insufficient material, or the 50-move rule draw.

VIEW
project() shows presentation data: board, viewer color, checked color, captured pieces, last move, move count, and white/black player ids and names. board is an 8x8 array. Pieces use K/Q/R/B/N/P and colors w/b. agentView prints your color, white/black names, side to move, checked color, last move, and algebraic coordinates.

OPPORTUNITIES
Only the side to move receives a turn opportunity with id:"turn", kind:"turn", prompt, and decision.type:"choose". choose.options are raw move decisions using long algebraic coordinate notation, for example {type:"move", from:"e2", to:"e4"}. Promotion choices add promotion:"Q", "R", "B", or "N". Castling and en passant appear as ordinary legal moves and are resolved automatically. The turn deadline expires to the first legal notation move decision.

APPLY
apply() validates and applies one legal move deterministically, updating castling rights, en passant target, captures, clocks, lastMove, and terminal status.

OUTCOME
outcome() returns null while playing. Checkmate returns {type:"winners", playerIds:[winner]}. Stalemate, insufficient material, and the 50-move rule return {type:"draw", playerIds:[]}.
